items.headline: Major commercial development planned on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial project is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, representing significant economic activity in San José's commercial corridor. This is the largest permitted project in this batch and could bring new businesses or expanded facilities to the area.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city council approved an outreach initiative offering amnesty to businesses that may be operating without proper licenses, targeting shopping centers specifically. This could affect local business compliance and city revenue from licensing fees.

items.headline: City unveils economic development incentives for retail and startups
items.summary: Cupertino introduced new incentive programs designed to attract and support retail businesses and startups in the area. This reflects city efforts to strengthen the local business ecosystem and potentially attract more commercial activity to South Bay.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up becomes official annual San José event
items.summary: The City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an annual city-sponsored special event on March 25, 2026. The city will now fund and coordinate this community cleanup initiative, and can accept donations of materials and services.

items.headline: City presents federal housing grant funding opportunities
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed applicants for Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25, which supports affordable housing and community development projects. This federal money could fund local housing initiatives or services for low-income residents.

items.headline: Palo Alto updates zoning and building rules for retail revival
items.summary: The Planning Commission is considering changes to zoning and building regulations to implement the city's retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. This could affect how retail businesses operate and where new commercial development is allowed in Palo Alto.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area transportation and land use study underway
items.summary: The Planning Commission is studying transportation and land use alternatives for the San Antonio Road area. This study could lead to changes in how that corridor develops and how traffic moves through that part of Palo Alto.

items.headline: Campbell updates beekeeping rules with new ordinance
items.summary: The city council adopted an updated beekeeping ordinance, allowing residents more flexibility in keeping bees on their properties. The changes reflect evolving local interest in backyard beekeeping and pollinator conservation.
